target.name: Artimus Robotics
target.founder: Eric Acome, Shane Mitchell, Nick Kellaris, Christoph Keplinger

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Artimus Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Eric Acome, Shane Mitchell, Nick Kellaris, and Christoph Keplinger. The company has raised $5M+ to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Valuation triangulation relied on a single applicable method due to data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach applied a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $5M total raised proxy, yielding an implied value of $12M. Peer-median analysis was not feasible; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied minimum threshold criteria (three or more datapoints) when filtered by stage, adjacent stage, or valuation tier. Applying all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable comparability noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and the limited comparable data available at the seed stage in this sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of stage-matched or near-stage peers with disclosed valuations, making it difficult to validate whether 2.5x represents an appropriate step-up multiplier for this specific company and market. Additional fundraising data from robotics-sector seed and early-growth rounds would strengthen conviction in this estimate.
